ul.li: The manifest is named by content hash.
Each chunk is named by content hash.
Identical bytes across versions → identical chunks (a one-line edit re-fetches one chunk, not the whole file).
Tamper any chunk or manifest → fails its content-address check.
Forge the pointer → fails the signature check.
Both failures exit non-zero with no traceback.

div.p: Mint an ed25519 keypair. private.key signs on the publisher. public.key is the pin a consumer trusts. Distributed out-of-band.
Chunk every file under --src/ with content-defined chunking (GearCDC). Write each unique chunk once under its sha256, build a manifest, sign a /latest version pointer.

Pull /latest. Verify signature against pinned pubkey, or fail closed. Diff manifest against local cache. Fetch only the missing chunks. Re-check each chunk's content hash. Atomically promote the new version.
Pure deterministic router picks the first registered runtime that ACCEPTs the Task. Same (Task, registry) → same runtime, always. Trace is replayable.
